INTRODUCTION
Throughout history the African continent has always been identified as a place with 
great, under exploited potential. Blessed with natural resources and huge reserves of fertile, unproductive land, surely Africa’s turn will come.
Today Africa is home to more than one billion people. By mid century that number 
will double to more than two billion people, 25% of the world total population.
Are those unexploited riches enough to provide for the sustenance and the 
economic growth for our people? Certainly not the mineral reserves, which already are per capita less than the global average. Agriculture will be a big growth area. Africa can feed the world but it cannot do so without developing it’s practices and people.
Where then are the riches that can sustain this population growth? The answer 
is contained in the question itself. It is the people of Africa that are our greatest resource and, in particular, the young people. Africa has the greatest proportion of young people in the world and Africa will succeed or fail entirely based on its ability to empower our young people to participate fully in the global economy.
Leaders throughout Africa, whether in Government, Commerce, Education, Health 
or the Development sector, must share the responsibility to grasp this opportunity to mobilise the innate talents of our people. 
“If you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together”.We at Techno Brain are committed and proud to do what we can.  Through education, 
employment, innovation and our solutions,  we  contribute to that shared goal.
Africa has the greatest proportion of young people in the world and Africa will succeed or fail entirely based on its ability to empower our young people to participate fully in the global economy

Techno Brain Foundation is a non-profit organization, 
founded with contributions from employees, directors and company profits, whose purpose is to empower the disadvantaged to take their place in Africa’s growth and 
help ensure that growth is environmentally sustainable.
In Africa today a growing number of young people are either lagging way behind because of absence of care, lack of educational funding or are already entangled with poverty. The Foundation’s program identifies young people  who have a promising academic lives and offers positive support systems to avoid the pitfalls that can derail them. The focus is slightly different at each level but the goal remains the same; empower the young person to make positive changes in his/her life and provide them an opportunity to grow. 
The Foundation’s program will leverage resources, 
facilities and personal contributions from Techno Brain’s global network and from partner organisations. It will develop purpose built facilities on a 10 acre campus to 
provide the best learning environment for students 
across the whole range of business disciplines.
But growth has its own costs in degradation of 
the natural environment. The Foundation’s 
other key priority is to fund programs 
to help ensure that the quality of the 
environment is sustained and improved. 
In association with environmental 
organisations the foundation will plant 50,000 trees over the next three years. It will also provide technology 
and technical assistance to those 
organisations protecting the welfare 
and protection of our wildlife to ensure 
our children and the children of the 
youths we are able to empower are able to 
enjoy the wonders of this special place.In a few decades, the relationship 
between the environment, resources 
and conflict may seem almost as 
obvious as the connection we see 
today between human rights, 
democracy and peace.
Wangari Maathai 
2004 Nobel Peace Prize Laureate
The foundation’s program identifies young people who have promising academic lives and offers positive support systems to avoid the pitfalls that can derail them. The foundation understands that the developmental level of critical life skills is paramount and it is our collective and individual responsibility to protect and nurture the global family and to preserve and tend to the environment in which we all live

Some of our CSR activities have included visiting and providing donations to orphanages, providing scholarships, sponsoring charity groups, supporting specific members of society such as women and children. • Knowledge Transfer - Techno Brain maintains a mutually collaborative relationship across various organizations, educational facilities and the public sector that helps enhance economic growth. Some of our academic advisors & partner programs are Microsoft for 
Africa, SAP Skill for Africa, Kenya Government driven ICT literacy among others. 
• Academic Alliance - Techno Brain seeks to establish centers of excellence at 
prestigious institutions of higher learning across Africa. The focus of the centers will be to offer for the first time in Africa, high end ICT training in the areas of banking, 
insurance, finance, marketing and human resources, using the latest state-of-

• Innovation - Techno Brain is committed to finding new and sophisticated

A WORD FROM  
THE GROUP CHAIRMAN
When I founded a small technology training company in Dar 
es Salaam in 1997, little did I ever imagine that less than 20 years later that company would have come so far and accomplished so much. 
That success is testament to the huge potential of Africa 
itself. We have achieved what we have by putting Africa first: by investing in and developing the most promising local talent and by having the confidence that solutions and services delivered by Africa, for Africa will provide the best value for customers in Africa.
But there is still more to do. The rest of the world has grown, 
now is the time for Africa to grow and we at Techno Brain have our part to play. Africa has the highest and fastest growing percentage of youth in the world. African women represent a huge and underutilised resource and engine of growth. By empowering these lives we will achieve success.We will continue to do our utmost to contribute what we can to the growth and success of this great continent and the personal fulfilment of all its people. I would like to 
personally thank the Techno Brain family, our customers 
and our business partners for making that possible.MAHESH R PATEL
A WORD FROM  
THE GROUP CEO
When I read the first drafts of this book it made me call to mind one of the 
often quoted observations of Bill Gates, who said “We always overestimate the change that will occur in the next two years and underestimate the change that will occur in the next ten”. 
Certainly in the world of technology those words are strikingly true. It was 
just over ten years ago that Facebook was founded. The first iPhone was still 3 years away. In Sub Saharan Africa less than 10% of the population had access to a mobile phone, availability of reliable high speed internet service was a pipe dream. Looking back on this review of Techno Brain’s achievements it is also clear how far we have come.
As a technology company, progress only comes through delivering real 
value to our customers, whether through enabling them to deliver better service to their customers, to improve economic performance, to support the health and welfare of the disadvantaged or to enable individuals to make the most of their own talents through education. As an African technology company enabling the youth to design and deliver such solutions is the only way to grow. Success is only achieved through true collaboration between supplier and customer and through hard work and determination. I would like to thank each and every one of our customers 
for the contribution they have made to our mutual success.
There are many customer stories in this volume, I hope you find them 
interesting, inspiring and useful in driving your own change over the years ahead.MANOJ SHANKER
The rest of the world has grown. Now it’s time for Africa. We are part of this growth by EMPOWERING LIVES
As an African technology company enabling the youth to design and deliver such solutions is the only way to grow

Technology solutions deployed in Africa are 
originally designed for developed markets, imported, and then made to fit for Africa. These solutions are neither tailor made for Africa, nor are they designed to face the continent’s unique challenges. 
At a macro level, these solutions might serve a 
purpose, but at ground level, they really don’t make a difference to the lives of Africans or help the continent make social and economic progress.  
Techno Brain has reversed this trend by developing 
Information & Communications Technology (ICT) solutions "For Africa, By Africa, In Africa" - where every solution developed for the continent is developed ground up in Africa, by the very TECHNO BRAIN PUTS  AFRICA FIRSTABOUT TECHNO BRAIN
same people who benefit from it. We work closely with governments, NGOs and the private sector to clearly understand the demographic, its unique challenges, the scale and the impact the project needs to create with the people.
The Guiding Principle of Techno Brain’s 
initiative is to create tailor made African solutions to complex processes, traditional challenges and opportunities,  sharpening the way Africa works, ensuring a long term economic stability and sustainability. Our Cutting edge innovations, technology and solutions are not bound by geography; we will continue to make inroads into global markets.The guiding principle of Techno Brain's initiative is to create ''Made in Africa" solutions that promote self-sustenance, social empowerment, long term economic stability and sustainability
